_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to solve linear equations that contain algebraic fractions.

_source.w: We are learning to solve linear equations that contain algebraic fractions.
y: Equations with algebraic fractions appear in many real-world models and in more advanced mathematics — solving them fluently is essential.
The strategy of multiplying through by the LCD to clear denominators is a powerful technique that simplifies complex equations.
These skills are directly applicable in physics, chemistry, economics and engineering.
eg: Solving: x/3 + x/4 = 7 by multiplying through by 12.
Solving: 3/(x–1) = 5/(x+2) by cross-multiplying and solving the resulting linear equation.
Checking solutions to ensure they do not make any denominator zero (excluded values).
Setting up and solving an equation with fractions from a rate problem: 'One pipe fills a tank in 6 hours, another in 4 hours. How long together?'
